title: Automations based on Items & Subitems
excerpt: I can easily ctreate an automation to do somethign when a Item’s status changes.  Or when a “sub item” status changes.  But that takes 2 automations.  Is there a way to make one so that if the status column (whihc is the…

posts.author: seandotson (Sean Dotson)
posts.cookedHtml:
I can easily ctreate an automation to do somethign when a Item’s status changes. Or when a “sub item” status changes. But that takes 2 automations. Is there a way to make one so that if the status column (whihc is the same in items and subitems) changes it fires the action.

Or do we have to create two?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: They’re different triggers so you’d need two automations

posts.author: kevinfantasymedia (Kevin - Fantasy Media LTD)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @seandotson

Good question. They’d have to be separate automations because the subitems’ statuses are independent.

I want to recommend this automation to you, hopefully it helps with what you’re trying to achieve →

image2294×712 40.1 KB

That automation is available in the Subitem Automations app. You can use the app with its free plan.

For follow up questions, email me anytime at kevin@fantasymedia.io !
